question_title: Will Thomas Massie file to run for any elective office before August 25, 2026?
summary: Massie lost his May 2026 House primary and is out of the 2026 House race; his only recent filing (2028 House) is out of cycle and pre-window. The only YES path is the speculative chain: McConnell vacates his seat → a special election is triggered with a filing deadline before Aug 25 → Massie decides to run and files (or files as a write-in). McConnell's office says he is 'improving' with no resignation indicated, no special election has been called, and the seat could legally 'sit empty for months.' Massie has explicitly not said he would run and is focused on 2028/presidential ambitions. Genuine health risk (hospitalized ~1 month at 84) and loud MTG/Bannon speculation keep a nonzero tail alive, but the status quo strongly favors NO across a short window.

factors.description: Massie lost his May 2026 House primary; no active 2026-cycle race requires a new filing. His only recent filing (2028 House) is out of cycle and before the window.

Only YES path is McConnell vacancy + special election with pre-Aug-25 filing deadline + Massie deciding to file, all in 6.5 weeks. Office says McConnell improving; no resignation; no election called; seat could sit empty for months.
Massie has explicitly NOT said he would run; focused on 2028/presidential ambitions. Even supporters call it a 'longshot theory'.
Genuine health risk (hospitalized ~1 month at 84, undisclosed), MTG/Bannon boosting the idea, and the write-in option keep a nonzero path open.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 5% (NO likely)**

Thomas Massie lost his May 2026 House primary, so he has no active 2026-cycle race requiring a new filing, and his only recent filing (2028 House) is out of cycle and predates the July 9 window. The sole realistic YES path is a compound chain — McConnell vacates his seat → a special election is triggered with a filing deadline before Aug 25 → Massie decides to run — all within ~6.5 weeks. McConnell's office reports he is "improving" with no resignation signaled, no special election has been called, and the seat could legally sit empty for months. Massie himself has not said he'd run. The active health speculation and write-in option keep a small tail alive, landing me at **5%**.
sources_consulted: [2026 United States Senate election in Kentucky - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States_Senate_election_in_Kentucky)
[2026 United States House of Representatives elections in Kentucky - Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States_House_of_Representatives_elections_in_Kentucky)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as **Yes** if, after July 9, 2026 and before August 25, 2026, [Thomas Massie](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thomas_Massie) files with any election authority to run for a public office in the 2026 election cycle. This may include, but is not limited to, any filing with the [FEC](https://www.fec.gov/help-candidates-and-committees/registering-candidate/house-senate-president-candidate-registration/) or any state filing such a declaration of intent to be a write-in candidate filed with the Commonwealth of Kentucky.
fine_print: This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/44544) which opened on 2026-07-10 09:00:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print of the linked original question. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.

factors.description: Massie lost his May 2026 House primary, so he has no active 2026-cycle race requiring a new filing. His only recent filing (2028 House) is out of cycle and pre-window.
Only YES path is McConnell vacancy + special election + Massie deciding to file, all within 6.5 weeks. McConnell's office says he is improving; no resignation indicated; no special election called.
Massie has explicitly NOT said he would run; he is focused on 2028/presidential ambitions. Even supporters frame it as a 'longshot theory'.
Genuine active speculation, real health risk (hospitalized ~1 month at 84, undisclosed condition), MTG/Bannon boosting the idea, plus write-in option keeps a nonzero path open.

input.anchor: Base rate for a specific politician making an unexpected new candidacy filing within a 6.5-week window absent any declared intent: ~5-8%.
input.assessment: Massie is out of the 2026 House race (lost primary). A new 2026-cycle filing essentially requires McConnell to vacate, a special election to be triggered with a filing deadline before Aug 25, AND Massie to decide to run — a compound chain of unlikely events in ~6.5 weeks. Status quo (no vacancy, no filing) strongly favors NO. Massie's own silence and 2028 focus cut hard against YES. Offsetting slightly: genuine health risk and active political speculation keep the tail alive. Rough chain estimate ~1.5-3%, bumped up for the write-in option and irreducible uncertainty. Settling near 4-5%.
input.calibration_notes: Status quo strongly favors NO. Not hedging toward 50 — evidence genuinely points low. Guarding against over-narrowing given real health uncertainty.
input.tool_audit: web_search returned rich, directly relevant results (Forbes, Fortune, Ballotpedia, FEC, local KY outlets). Confirmed primary loss, 2028 filing, McConnell hospitalization status. No tool failures.
input.process_reflection: Standard scaffolding worked well for a low-probability compound-event question. Web search was sufficient; no need for subforecast decomposition given the clear status-quo-NO structure. Main risk is over-narrowing on a genuinely uncertain health situation.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
All four factors are grounded in the research trace. I verified each core claim:

- Factor 1 (primary loss + 2028 out-of-cycle filing): Confirmed by Forbes ("lost his May primary to a Trump-backed challenger") and Spectrum News ("filed with the FEC to run for the same House seat in 2028"). The 2028 filing was late May — correctly excluded as pre-window (window starts July 9) and wrong cycle. Negative logit is correct-direction (no active 2026 race → NO).
- Factor 2 (McConnell vacancy chain): Confirmed — hospitalized since June 14, office says "continues to improve," no resignation indicated, 2024 KY law mandates special election. Fortune even notes the seat "could sit empty for months," which supports the low probability. Negative logit correct.
- Factor 3 (Massie silent, 2028/presidential focus): Confirmed — Spectrum News "Massie keeps options open for 2028," concession-speech chants of "2028"/"president." No Senate-run statement found. Negative logit correct.
- Factor 4 (health risk, MTG/Bannon boosting, write-in path): Confirmed — Washington Examiner (MTG "would love to see"), Forbes (Bannon), 84-year-old ~1 month hospitalized, undisclosed condition. Positive logit correct (only YES-supporting factor).

No hallucination, no double-counting, no wrong-direction factors, no assessment/factor contradiction.

Resolution criteria are PROVIDED (not missing) and the factors engage with the specific terms — FEC filing (Factor 1) and Kentucky write-in option (Factor 4) are both explicitly addressed, and the July 9–Aug 25 window is respected. No pre-publication event resolves the question.

Worldview store contains no Massie/McConnell entries — no cross-question or binary/numeric consistency issue.

Probability check: The anchor (5–8% for an unexpected new candidacy filing absent declared intent) is reasonable. The compound YES chain (McConnell vacates → special election with a pre-Aug-25 filing deadline → Massie decides to file) is genuinely unlikely in 6.5 weeks, and the sources note filing deadlines are 49–56 days out with the seat potentially empty "for months," further compressing the odds. The write-in path keeps a small tail alive. My independent estimate lands in the 3–6% range; the agent's 5% is well-calibrated. No meaningful divergence, and the final probability sits inside the well-sourced anchor.

Counterargument is strong (coherent alternative story with specific evidentiary basis, not a token restatement). Self-confidence of 0.82 is appropriate.

Minor issue (does not affect forecast): The tool_audit narrative claims "No tool failures," but ground-truth metrics show 2 errors (premortem 100%, reflection 50%). The trace itself acknowledges "There was a parameter formatting error" on the premortem. The evidence-producing tools (web_search, wikipedia) had 0% errors, so the research chain is clean — this is a cosmetic inaccuracy, not an evidence problem. Not sufficient to warrant a warn given the forecast is otherwise sound.
